metadata.greeting: Get ready for a fun-filled weekend in London! There are many exciting activities for a 3-year-old, from exploring sea creatures at the Horniman Museum's soft play to meeting farm animals at Spitalfields City Farm. Plus, plenty of free museum activities and outdoor adventures await!

activities.title: KIDS CARIBBEAN CARNIVAL + K POP - Just Vibez FAMILY FETE!
activities.description: A family-friendly event with Caribbean Carnival and K-Pop influences.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a vibrant and engaging experience with music and dance, suitable for active toddlers.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Clapham Junction, then a short bus ride to Brixton.
transport.tip: Check the event details for specific age recommendations and activities.

activities.title: Last Unicorn Airways
activities.description: Join two playful baggage handlers as they unpack a globe-trotting adventure from a magical final flight, where every suitcase tells a story.
activities.whyItWorks: Imaginative storytelling and engaging performance suitable for young children.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Shadwell, then a short walk to the theatre.
transport.tip: All performances at Half Moon are relaxed performances, and blind and visually impaired audiences can pre-book touch tours.

activities.title: Cut A Shine St. Patrick's Day – Family Barn Dance
activities.description: Dances designed for the very smallest kids through to a couple of proper ones for the grownups, plus meat or vegan Irish stew and a kids' disco to finish.
activities.whyItWorks: Active and engaging with music, dance, and food, perfect for a family outing.

transport.fromHackney: Located in Hackney; easily accessible by bus or walking.
transport.tip: Check the website for specific times and ticket prices.

activities.title: Library Club at the Hackney Showroom
activities.description: With reading corner and diverse book-pairing service.
activities.whyItWorks: Provides a relaxed and engaging environment for toddlers to explore books and stories.

transport.fromHackney: Located in Hackney; easily accessible by bus or walking.
transport.tip: Check term times before visiting.

activities.title: Planet Play at Chickenshed
activities.description: Interactive, sensory show.
activities.whyItWorks: Specifically designed for young children, offering a stimulating sensory experience.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Seven Sisters, then the Victoria Line to Southgate.
transport.tip: Must have a ticket, even for free babies.

activities.title: Spitalfields City Farm
activities.description: A hidden gem near Brick Lane with a community feel. Check out their line up of Easter activities.
activities.whyItWorks: Kids love animals! A chance to see goats, pigs, chickens, and more.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Shoreditch High Street, then a short walk.
transport.tip: Check their website for special Easter activities.

activities.title: Young V&A
activities.description: A vibrant and interactive museum designed specifically for children and families with hands-on activities, workshops, and exhibitions.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a range of activities to spark creativity and imagination in young children.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Shoreditch High Street, then the Central Line to Bethnal Green.
transport.tip: Check the website for specific workshops and events.

activities.title: Cinema Chill: Peace at Last
activities.description: Relaxed cinema experience designed for young children.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a gentle and calming introduction to cinema for toddlers.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Shadwell, then a short walk to the theatre.
transport.tip: All performances at Half Moon are relaxed performances.

activities.title: Natural History Museum
activities.description: Explore nature, discover fascinating creatures and intriguing science, and enjoy family-friendly galleries.
activities.whyItWorks: Dinosaurs, interactive exhibits, and a wide range of fascinating displays to capture a child's imagination.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Highbury & Islington, then the Victoria Line to South Kensington.
transport.tip: Download the Museum map to plan your visit.

activities.title: Science Museum
activities.description: Explore iconic objects created over the past 250 years, from Apollo 10 to the first Apple computer.
activities.whyItWorks: Interactive exhibits, space exploration, and a dedicated children's area.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Highbury & Islington, then the Victoria Line to South Kensington.
transport.tip: Check the website for specific family activities and events.

activities.title: Horniman Museum and Gardens
activities.description: Explore natural history, musical instruments, and a beautiful garden with animals and a play area.
activities.whyItWorks: A mix of indoor and outdoor activities, plus animals and interactive exhibits.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Forest Hill.
transport.tip: Check out the Under the Sea Soft Play for younger children (paid entry).

activities.title: Museum of London Docklands
activities.description: Learn about the history of London's waterfront through interactive exhibits and displays.
activities.whyItWorks: Interactive exhibits and a Mudlarks play area for younger children.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Shoreditch High Street, then the DLR to West India Quay.
transport.tip: Visit the Mudlarks Gallery, an interactive gallery designed for children.

activities.title: Sky Garden
activities.description: A stunning indoor garden with panoramic views of the city.
activities.whyItWorks: Sensory experience with greenery and plants, plus amazing views.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Shoreditch High Street, then the Circle or District Line to Monument.
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance, released on Mondays, around three weeks in advance.

activities.title: Golders Hill Park Zoo
activities.description: A small zoo in the Hampstead Heath area with rare species of birds and animals.
activities.whyItWorks: Free entry and a variety of animals in a park setting.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Gospel Oak, then a short bus ride to Golders Green.
transport.tip: Combine with a visit to Hampstead Heath.

activities.title: British Library
activities.description: The UK's National Library with regular sessions for under 5s.
activities.whyItWorks: Regular sessions for under 5s. Beautiful and awe-inspiring.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Highbury & Islington, then the Victoria Line to Euston.
transport.tip: Check website for specific times and events.

activities.title: I Want My Hat Back Trilogy
activities.description: Jon Klassen's beloved books, I Want My Hat Back, This is Not My Hat and We Found A Hat, have found a new life of their own LIVE on stage.
activities.whyItWorks: Puppetry show based on popular children's books, perfect for young children.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Highbury & Islington, then a short bus ride to Islington.
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance, especially for weekend performances.

activities.title: Sneaky Squirrel's Chocolate Chase at Pickled Pepper Books
activities.description: Immersive adventure for Easter.
activities.whyItWorks: Interactive and engaging, perfect for younger children.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Finsbury Park, then a bus to Crouch End.
transport.tip: Book in advance.

activities.title: A Midsummer Night's Dream
activities.description: A theatrical performance of Shakespeare's A Midsummer Night's Dream.
activities.whyItWorks: An accessible introduction to Shakespeare for older children.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Shoreditch High Street, then a train to London Bridge. Walk to Tooley Street.
transport.tip: Check the Unicorn Theatre website for age recommendations and showtimes.

activities.title: Lindt Gold Bunny Hunt at Hampton Court Palace
activities.description: Roam around the gardens and find hidden Lindt Gold Bunny statues to win a chocolate treat.
activities.whyItWorks: An outdoor activity that encourages exploration and offers a sweet reward.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Stratford, then the Jubilee Line to Waterloo, then a train to Hampton Court.
transport.tip: Dress for wind and bring snacks.

activities.title: Octonauts Above and Beyond at Kew Gardens
activities.description: An interactive trail that invites young explorers to complete challenges, discover facts and learn how to protect our planet.
activities.whyItWorks: Combines outdoor exploration with learning and interactive challenges.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Richmond.
transport.tip: Allow 2-3 hours for the activity.

activities.title: Royal Mews Easter Egg Expedition
activities.description: Follow an Easter trail around the working stables at Buckingham Palace.
activities.whyItWorks: A unique opportunity to explore a royal location and participate in an Easter-themed activity.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Highbury & Islington, then the Victoria Line to Green Park. Walk to Royal Mews.
transport.tip: Look out for clues as you explore the historic carriages and courtyard.

activities.title: Zoonormous Egg Hunt at London Zoo
activities.description: Wander past giraffes, lions, monkeys and more incredible wildlife as you search for hidden eggs on a special Easter egg hunt.
activities.whyItWorks: Combines animal viewing with an Easter egg hunt.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Highbury & Islington, then the Victoria Line to Regent's Park.
transport.tip: Pick up a trail card and crack a secret password.
